question: What will be Russia's annual GDP growth rate for Q2 2026?

factors.description: Q2 2026 monthly GDP YoY April +1.3%, May +0.3% (avg ~0.8%), decelerating within quarter
CBR characterizes growth as 'moderate after temporary decline at start of year', implying positive Q2 rebound from Q1 -0.2%
Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4-1.1%; with Q1 at -0.2, later quarters must average higher
Sharp deceleration momentum (1.3->0.3 monthly) creates downside risk of near-flat or negative quarter

summary: Q2 2026 Russia GDP annual (YoY) growth per Trading Economics. Q2 covers Apr-Jun 2026. Monthly GDP YoY: April +1.3%, May +0.3% (avg ~0.8%, decelerating); June unreleased. Quarterly YoY trajectory decelerated through 2025 (+1.4/+1.1/+0.6/+1.0) then hit -0.2% in Q1 2026 (first contraction since 2023). CBR describes Q2 as "moderate growth after the temporary decline at the start of the year." Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4-1.1% (Ministry 0.4, WB 0.8, CBR 0.5-1.5, IMF 1.1). A scenario mixture (60% base ~0.5%, 25% deeper-deceleration downside, 15% upside from base effects/monthly-quarterly divergence) yields median ~0.5%, P10 -0.05%, P90 ~1.08%, with ~12% probability of a negative print.
reasoning:
Forecast submitted for Russia's Q2 2026 annual GDP growth rate.

**Summary:** Median estimate **~0.5%**, with an 80% CI of roughly **-0.05% to +1.08%**.

**Key reasoning:**
- Q2 2026 covers April–June. Monthly GDP YoY came in at **+1.3% (April)** and **+0.3% (May)** — averaging ~0.8% but decelerating hard.
- The quarterly trajectory decelerated all through 2025 (+1.4/+1.1/+0.6/+1.0) then hit **-0.2% in Q1 2026**, the first contraction since 2023.
- The CBR describes Q2 as "moderate growth after the temporary decline at the beginning of the year," implying a weak positive rebound.
- Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4–1.1% (Ministry 0.4, World Bank 0.8, CBR 0.5–1.5, IMF 1.1).

I used a scenario mixture (60% base ~0.5%, 25% deeper-deceleration downside, 15% upside) to produce the distribution, placing ~12% probability on a negative print. The premortem reviewer approved, noting only that a marginal upward nudge to the center could be justified by the historical tendency of Russian quarterly figures to diverge upward from soft monthly reads.
